sections.heading: 1. Short title
sections.text: This Act may be cited as the Merchant Shipping (Temporary Provisions) Act.

sections.heading: 2. Interpretation
sections.text: In this Act and in the application ofthe Codeby this Act, unless the context otherwise requires—"the Code" means the Merchant Shipping Acts, 1894 to 1965, of the United Kingdom, and these Acts to the extent of their application by this Act to Zambia shall be construed as one Act;"person qualified", in relation to the ownership of a Zambianship, includes any person wholly owning ashipdeclared to be a Zambianshipunder section five;"principal Act" means the Merchant Shipping Act, 1894, of the United Kingdom, and any amendments as part ofthe Codeapplied by this Act;"Registrar of Shipping" has the meaning assigned thereto in section seven;"related Acts" means any part ofthe Codeas applied by this Act, but does not include theprincipal Act;"Representative of Zambia" means any person having diplomatic or consular status and acting in that behalf for Zambia;"ship" includes vessel.

sections.heading: 3. Application of Merchant Shipping Acts, 1894 to 1965
sections.text: Subject to the provisions of this Act, the Merchant Shipping Acts, 1894 to 1965, of the United Kingdom (in this Act referred to as "the Code") to the extent set out in the Schedule shallmutatis mutandisapply and be part of the law of Zambia.

sections.heading: 4. Application of Part I of Merchant Shipping Act, 1894
sections.text: The application of Part I of theprincipal Actas part ofthe Codeshall be read subject to the provisions of sections five to eleven of this Act.

sections.heading: 5. Zambianship
sections.text: a person who is resident in Zambia;

sections.heading: 6. Registration of Zambianship
sections.text: Every Zambianshipshall, unless exempted from registry, be registered under this Act at such place in Zambia or elsewhere as the Minister may, by statutory order, designate and such place so designated shall be the port of registry of suchshipfor the purposes of this Act.

sections.heading: 7.Registrar of Shipping
sections.text: References to registration underthe Codeshall be read as references to registration under this Part, and theRegistrar of Shipping, for the purposes of this Act, shall be such public officer as the Minister shall, byGazettenotice, designate asRegistrar of Shipping.

sections.heading: 8. Restriction of powers of mortgagee
sections.text: The power of sale conferred upon a mortgagee under theprincipal Actshall not be exercised without the leave of the High Court.

sections.heading: 9. Zambian flag
sections.text: References inthe Codeto the British flag, and red ensign or any other flag permitted, shall be read and construed exclusively as references to the National Flag of Zambia or to any other flag authorised by the Minister, by statutory order, for use on anyship.

sections.heading: 10. Provisional registration of Zambianship
sections.text: Where ashipis purchased outside Zambia, the bill of sale may be deposited with aRepresentative of Zambia, who shall forthwith notify theRegistrar of Shipping, and when deposited with theRepresentative of Zambiaunder this section theshipshall, for a period of six months, be deemed to be provisionally registered under this Part:Provided that registration under this section may, upon application by the owner, be extended by the Minister for one further period of six months.

sections.heading: 11. Registration of mortgages executed overseas
sections.text: Any mortgage for the purpose of this Act executed outside Zambia may be deposited with aRepresentative of Zambia. TheRepresentative of Zambiashall, where he is satisfied that the mortgage appears to be in proper order and fully executed, and on payment of the registration fee, forthwith notify theRegistrar of Shippingof the deposit of the mortgage and the names of the parties with the amount secured, and the mortgage and the registration fee shall be posted to theRegistrar of Shipping.

sections.heading: 12. Execution of instruments
sections.text: if executed in Zambia, be attested in accordance with the laws of Zambia;

sections.heading: 13. Application of Part II of Merchant Shipping Act, 1894, etc.
sections.text: The application of Part II of theprincipal Actandrelated Actsas part ofthe Codeshall be read subject to the provisions of sections fourteen to eighteen of this Act.

sections.heading: 14. Certificates of competency
sections.text: TheRegistrar of Shippingmay, where he is satisfied, accept certificates of competency issued by any authority outside Zambia, and where so accepted a certificate of competency shall for all the purposes of this Act be recognised as valid in Zambia and be deemed to have been issued under this Act:Provided that nothing in this section shall prohibit the issue in Zambia of a certificate of competency in any proper case.

sections.heading: 15. Reference to certain Commissioners not to apply
sections.text: References to the National Debt Commissioners shall have no application in Zambia.

sections.heading: 16. Seaman's book
sections.text: TheRegistrar of Shippingmay issue a book (in this section called a seaman's book) for use under this Act in a form approved by the Minister, or may approve any seaman's book issued by any competent authority outside Zambia.

sections.heading: 17. Foreign medical reports on certain seamen
sections.text: In the case of a foreign seaman, theRegistrar of Shippingmay accept a report from any foreign medical inspector appearing to him to be duly qualified where it relates to the fitness of that foreign seaman for duty at sea.

sections.heading: 18. Extended application of agreements with crew
sections.text: References in sections 113 and 115 of theprincipal Actto agreements with the crew shall, where the case so requires, be deemed to refer to more than one agreement in respect of any oneship.

sections.heading: 19. Application of Part V of Merchant Shipping Act, 1894, etc.
sections.text: The application of Part V of theprincipal Actandrelated Actsas part ofthe Codeshall be read subject to the provisions of sections twenty to twenty-five of this Act.

sections.heading: 20. Application of collision regulations and rules
sections.text: Rules made under the provisions of section 418 of theprincipal Actand in force at the commencement of this Act shall be deemed to have been made by the Minister under this Part.

sections.heading: 21. Application of load line rules
sections.text: Rules made under the provisions of section 42 of the Merchant Shipping (Safety and Load Line Conventions) Act, 1932, of the United Kingdom, and in force on the 9th May, 1967, shall be deemed to have been made by the Minister under this Part.

sections.heading: 22. Application of rules relating to construction, etc.
sections.text: Rules made under the provisions of section 1, 2, 3, 5 or 6 of the Merchant Shipping (Safety Convention) Act, 1949, of the United Kingdom, and in force on the 9th June, 1964, shall be deemed to have been made by the Minister under this Part.

sections.heading: 23. Application of tonnage regulations and load line rules
sections.text: Regulations made under section 1, and rules made pursuant to section 2, of the Merchant Shipping Act, 1965, of the United Kingdom, and in force on the 9th May, 1967, shall be deemed to have been made by the Minister under this Part.

sections.heading: 24. Adherence to international conventions
sections.text: Whenever the Government of Zambia adheres to any international convention relating to merchant shipping, the Minister may do all things necessary to give effect thereto and for such purposes the Minister may, by statutory instrument, make rules and regulations.

sections.heading: 25. Power to issue certificates of compliance with Safety Conventions
sections.text: Where a power is conferred to issue certificates under the Merchant Shipping (Safety Convention) Act, 1949, of the United Kingdom, the power may be exercised by the Minister or by any person authorised in that behalf by the Minister.

sections.heading: 26. Application of Part VI of Merchant Shipping Act, 1894
sections.text: The application of Part VI of theprincipal Actas part ofthe Codeshall be read subject to the provisions of sections twenty-seven and twenty-eight of this Act.

sections.heading: 27. Rules as to investigations and inquiries
sections.text: The power conferred upon the Lord Chancellor by section 479 of theprincipal Actmay be exercised by the Minister:Provided that until rules are made for Zambia under the provisions of this Act any rules made and in force on the commencement of this Act shallmutatis mutandisbe deemed to have been made under this Act, and the provisions of subsection (3) of section 479 of theprincipal Actshall have no application to any rules so made and in force.

sections.heading: 28. Rules for procedure of court of survey, etc.
sections.text: The power conferred upon the Lord Chancellor by section 489 of theprincipal Actmay be exercised by the Chief Justice:Provided that until rules are made for Zambia under the provisions of this Act any rules made and in force on the commencement of this Act shallmutatis mutandisbe deemed to have been made under this Act.

sections.heading: 29. Power for Minister to order an inquiry upon request
sections.text: Where any government or competent authority so requests, the Minister may order an inquiry into any shipping casualty.

sections.heading: 30. Application of Part VIII of Merchant Shipping Act, 1894, etc.
sections.text: Part VIII of theprincipal Actas part ofthe Codeandrelated Actsshall be read without modification other than this Act.

sections.heading: 31. Application of Part XIII of Merchant Shipping Act, 1894
sections.text: The application of Part XIII of theprincipal Actas part ofthe Codeshall be read without modification other than this Act.

sections.heading: 32. Application of Part XIV of Merchant Shipping Act, 1894, etc.
sections.text: The application of Part XIV of theprincipal Actandrelated Actsas part ofthe Codeshall be read without modification other than this Act.

sections.heading: 33. Power to appoint, etc.,shipsurveyors
sections.text: The Minister may appoint and remove surveyors of ships for any of the purposes of this Act.

sections.heading: 34. Power for Minister to exempt persons or things
sections.text: Where there is power to exempt any person or thing from all or any of the provisions of this Act or ofthe Code, as applied by this Act, the Minister may by writing under his hand exercise that power.

sections.heading: 35. Delegation of power
sections.text: The Minister in his discretion may by writing under his hand delegate all or any of his powers under this Act or ofthe Code, as applied by this Act, to theRegistrar of Shippingor any other person:Provided that nothing in this section shall apply to the power to make any statutory instrument for the purposes ofthe Code, as applied by this Act.

sections.heading: 36. Power exercisable by Order in Council may be exercised by order
sections.text: The President may make statutory orders under this Act for the purposes ofthe Code, as applied by this Act, and references to any power to be exercised by Order in Council shall, unless otherwise prescribed by this Act, be read as references to any statutory order made by the President.

sections.heading: 37. Prescribed fees
sections.text: Fees payable under any part ofthe Codeapplied by this Act shall, unless otherwise provided under this Act, be the fees payable underthe Codeand in force on the commencement of this Act.
